title: selaginella longiaristata
intro: Selaginella longiaristata, also known as 'Maidenhair Fern', is a type of terrestrial plant belonging to the Selaginellaceae family, characterized by its unique, long-stemmed and strap-like leaves. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, reducing cholesterol levels, and acting as an antiseptic, as well as having antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Selaginella longiaristata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
